ArchitectureNotes.objective: Keep the template usable as a production-grade starting point while making GRACE the primary agent workflow contract.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: Do not mirror every private helper in shared XML artifacts; use shared docs for public module boundaries and verification references.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Admin GraphQL schema and generated artifacts must be changed together.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Root Nx targets span Go, TypeScript, Docker-backed services, and generated code, so verification must be scoped explicitly.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Legacy agent-docs commands must not be reintroduced into active agent guidance.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-4: Coverage gates must distinguish generated or bootstrap code from handwritten behavior using an explicit allowlist and replacement verification.

contract.purpose: Govern agent operating rules, shared GRACE artifacts, and file-local semantic markup for meaningful repository changes.

param.name: shared-artifacts
param.type: docs/requirements.xml, docs/technology.xml, docs/development-plan.xml, docs/knowledge-graph.xml, docs/verification-plan.xml, docs/operational-packets.xml

param.name: governed-files
param.type: New or meaningfully edited source, test, tooling, config, script, CI, Docker, and durable documentation files outside docs/*.xml

param.name: file-local-contracts
param.type: MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, function/block anchors, and CHANGE_SUMMARY markup where applicable

param.name: shared-contract-sync
param.type: Updated module, graph, verification, and packet contracts

export-agent-guide.PURPOSE: Route agents through GRACE skills, docs-first workflow, focused verification, and file-local markup rules.

export-file-local-contract.PURPOSE: Require MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, semantic anchors, and CHANGE_SUMMARY for new or meaningfully edited governed files without requiring repo-wide historical backfill.

export-grace-change-flow.PURPOSE: Define the read-contracts, patch-scope, implement, verify, refresh, and lint workflow.

export-operational-packets.PURPOSE: Provide worker packet, graph delta, verification delta, and failure handoff expectations.

target.tests: xmllint, grace lint, and focused diff checks for agent-governance changes

notes.note-1: Historical unmarked files may remain unmarked until touched by a meaningful change.

contract.purpose: Provide root package scripts, Nx project graph, and shared target defaults for all apps and libraries.

param.name: workspace-config
param.type: package.json, nx.json, project.json files

param.name: commands
param.type: Nx and Bun command surface

export-bun-scripts.PURPOSE: Expose local web-stack dev, build, test, lint, SQL/API/web codegen, and coverage scripts.

export-nx-targets.PURPOSE: Expose per-project build, serve, test, lint, codegen, and e2e targets.

export-coverage-scripts.PURPOSE: Expose test:coverage, test:e2e, and verify:coverage root scripts.

target.tests: Root target execution through Nx commands

notes.note-1: Keep root scripts thin wrappers over Nx target execution.

M-COVERAGE-GATE.NAME: CoverageGate

contract.purpose: Enforce the 100 percent coverage and full-stack e2e contract during explicit coverage, release, and template handoff phases.

param.name: generated-allowlist
param.type: gqlgen output, sqlc users/admin query output, web-admin GraphQL output, and bootstrap entrypoints listed in tools/coverage/coverage.config.json

param.name: typescript-summaries
param.type: dist/coverage/web-admin/coverage-summary.json, dist/coverage/web/coverage-summary.json, and dist/coverage/tools/coverage-summary.json

param.name: e2e-services
param.type: Docker Compose PostgreSQL and Redis plus local API and web servers

param.name: test-infrastructure
param.type: docker/docker-compose.test.yml PostgreSQL and Redis services

param.name: playwright-artifacts
param.type: dist/test-results/web-admin-e2e, dist/test-results/web-e2e, dist/playwright-report/web-admin, and dist/playwright-report/web

export-run.PURPOSE: Run Go, web-admin, web, workspace tooling, and codegen coverage checks.

export-preflight.PURPOSE: Validate coverage config and start Docker e2e dependencies.

export-playwright-config.PURPOSE: Run real API and browser checks from apps/web-admin/e2e and apps/web/e2e.

export-test-infrastructure.PURPOSE: Start isolated PostgreSQL and Redis services for coverage and e2e gates.

export-safe-target-guards.PURPOSE: Fail destructive tests before cleanup when the database target is not `monorepo_test`.

M-COVERAGE-GATE.depends: M-WORKSPACE, M-GRAPHQL-SCHEMA, M-API, M-WEB-ADMIN, M-WEB, M-BOT

target.tests: bun run test:coverage, bunx nx run web-admin:e2e, and bunx nx run web:e2e

M-CI-CD.NAME: GitLabDokployCICD

contract.purpose: Validate merge requests, route jobs to the department runner pool, run release gates, build service images including the public Next `web` image, create GitLab releases, and trigger Dokploy Docker Compose deployments.

param.name: git-refs
param.type: merge requests, develop, main, and SemVer tags

param.name: ci-variables
param.type: GitLab registry and Dokploy environment variables

param.name: runner-pool
param.type: department GitLab group runners with fast, full, image-build, and deploy tags

param.name: dockerfiles
param.type: docker/api.Dockerfile, docker/web.Dockerfile, docker/bot.Dockerfile

param.name: pipeline-evidence
param.type: coverage, e2e, image metadata, release, and deployment artifacts

param.name: dokploy-deployments
param.type: dev and production Dokploy compose deployments with WEB_API_BASE_URL for the public Next web service

export-gitlab-pipeline.PURPOSE: Expose GitLab CI stages and jobs.

export-ci-helpers.PURPOSE: Expose tested helper commands for affected base, release tag validation, image metadata, and Dokploy deployment.

export-dokploy-template.PURPOSE: Expose environment-driven Dokploy compose stack template.

export-runner-pool.PURPOSE: Expose department GitLab runner classes, tags, concurrency, and Docker-in-Docker guardrails.

M-CI-CD.depends: M-WORKSPACE, M-COVERAGE-GATE, M-API, M-WEB-ADMIN, M-WEB, M-BOT

target.tests: bunx nx run ci-tools:test and CI helper command smoke tests

contract.purpose: Load, merge, and validate Go application configuration from files and environment variables.

param.name: config-files
param.type: YAML and env files

param.name: environment
param.type: process environment

param.name: config
param.type: validated Go config structs

contract.purpose: Provide shared zap logger construction, request context propagation, and HTTP middleware logging.

param.name: request-context
param.type: context.Context and HTTP request metadata

param.name: logger
param.type: zap logger and request-scoped fields

export-Middleware.PURPOSE: Attach request logging and request IDs.

contract.purpose: Define the admin GraphQL schema used by API gqlgen generation and web-admin client generation, including admin auth operations.

param.name: schema
param.type: validated GraphQL schema

param.name: generated-artifacts
param.type: API resolvers and web-admin client types

export-Query.PURPOSE: Reference user queries and current-admin `me` operation.

export-Mutation.PURPOSE: Reference user mutations plus `loginAdmin`, `logoutAdmin`, and `createAdmin` operations.

export-UserTypes.PURPOSE: Reference user domain types and result unions.

export-AdminAuthTypes.PURPOSE: Admin identity, login/logout/create result unions, and auth error contracts.

contract.purpose: Serve HTTP health, protected admin GraphQL, and public REST endpoints with PostgreSQL, Redis admin sessions, credentialed CORS/origin guards, goose migrations, sqlc-generated user/admin queries, and shared service logic.

param.name: http-requests
param.type: health, credentialed admin GraphQL, and public REST HTTP requests

param.name: config
param.type: validated API config

param.name: responses
param.type: HTTP, GraphQL, and REST JSON responses

export-main.PURPOSE: Wire config, logger, storage, admin bootstrap, session middleware, REST routes, GraphQL server, and graceful shutdown.

export-user-service.PURPOSE: Expose shared transport-neutral user domain logic.

export-user-repository.PURPOSE: Expose PostgreSQL users repository adapter backed by sqlc-generated pgx/v5 queries.

export-admin-auth-service.PURPOSE: Expose first-admin seed, login, current admin, create-admin, and logout behavior.

export-admin-repository.PURPOSE: Expose PostgreSQL admin_users repository adapter backed by sqlc-generated pgx/v5 queries.

export-admin-session-store.PURPOSE: Expose Redis-backed opaque admin session storage using HMAC-derived keys.

export-admin-auth-middleware.PURPOSE: Expose admin principal context, cookie bridge, session lookup, and origin guard middleware.

export-user-resolvers.PURPOSE: Expose generated admin user query and mutation resolvers protected by admin session context.

export-admin-auth-resolvers.PURPOSE: Expose loginAdmin, logoutAdmin, me, and createAdmin GraphQL resolvers.

notes.note-1: TODO(product-auth): Web-admin admin sessions authenticate operators for template admin GraphQL, but downstream products must still define owner/tenant scoping and per-resource authorization where the product requires it.

contract.purpose: Provide the Vite React Router web-admin SPA, credentialed generated GraphQL client, approved shadcn-based UI-kit surface, and adapted sidebar-07 app shell for the admin user flow.

param.name: browser-events
param.type: React UI interactions

param.name: graphql-api
param.type: credentialed admin API GraphQL endpoint at /graphql

param.name: config
param.type: VITE_GRAPHQL_API_URL and VITE_APP_NAME

param.name: ui-state
param.type: Rendered shell, route pages, sidebar state, breadcrumbs, user list, theme state, and mutation results

param.name: ui-kit
param.type: @shared/ui primitives, sidebar-07 app shell compositions, admin layout compositions, theme toggle, and /ui-kit reference page

export-routes.PURPOSE: Expose Vite React Router routes, provider wiring, and app shell.

export-generated-client.PURPOSE: Expose generated GraphQL operation types and cookie credential transport.

export-admin-auth-operations.PURPOSE: Expose loginAdmin, logoutAdmin, CurrentAdmin, and createAdmin operation documents for generated types.

export-AtlasNutritionApiAdapter.PURPOSE: Expose raw /graphql/atlas nutrition adapter functions, local TypeScript result types, and normalized typed result errors without changing admin GraphQL client semantics.

export-AdminAuthFrontend.PURPOSE: Expose CurrentAdmin-backed auth provider, login page, protected route layout, safe return-to handling, and sidebar logout for the web-admin app.

export-LoginRoute.PURPOSE: Render `/login` outside the sidebar shell and authenticate through cookie-backed `loginAdmin`.

export-ProtectedRoutes.PURPOSE: Protect all non-login admin routes through `CurrentAdmin` before rendering the global admin shell.

export-ui-kit.PURPOSE: Expose approved Radix Rhea shadcn primitives, sidebar-07 shell compositions, zinc/sidebar base tokens, indigo theme accents, theme toggle, and admin layout compositions through @shared/ui.

export-ui-kit-reference.PURPOSE: Expose /ui-kit as the visible component reference route for future admin pages.

target.tests: apps/web-admin test, coverage, and e2e targets

contract.purpose: Provide the Next.js App Router public web UI, independent local UI-kit primitives, same-origin REST route proxy, and REST users client for the public reference user flow.

param.name: browser-events
param.type: React UI interactions

param.name: ui-kit
param.type: apps/web @shared/ui primitives independent from web-admin

param.name: rest-api
param.type: public API REST endpoints under /api/users via Next route handlers

param.name: config
param.type: WEB_API_BASE_URL for Next server/runtime route proxy code

param.name: ui-state
param.type: Rendered pages, user list, theme state, and REST mutation results

export-next-app.PURPOSE: Expose Next App Router layout, page, client component, and route handlers.

export-rest-client.PURPOSE: Expose typed REST users client and config parsing.

export-ui-kit.PURPOSE: Expose independent Radix Rhea shadcn-compatible public web primitives with Tailwind v4 semantic zinc base tokens, indigo theme accents, and theme toggle through apps/web @shared/ui.

target.tests: apps/web test, coverage, typecheck, build, and e2e targets

contract.purpose: Run a Telegram bot entry point with config, shared logging, middleware, and command handlers.

param.name: telegram-updates
param.type: Telegram bot update stream

param.name: config
param.type: validated bot config

param.name: bot-responses
param.type: Telegram messages and handler side effects

export-main.PURPOSE: Wire bot config, logger, middleware, handlers, and graceful shutdown.

export-handlers.PURPOSE: Expose start, help, and default command handlers.

contract.purpose: Provide product background, glossary, journeys, personas, business rules, and data dictionary context for planning.

param.name: planning-context
param.type: Human-readable product context for requirements and GRACE planning

notes.note-1: Product docs are context; `docs/requirements.xml` remains the executable requirements contract for agents.

contract.purpose: Provide the project-local Codex skill whose main controller launches one scope-orchestrator per product review scope, records source deltas for new docs or answered questions, runs the consistency scope only after primary scope aggregation, stages structure under `.tasks`, and synthesizes source-backed facts plus approved derivations into `docs/product-verified` only after the approval gate.

param.name: source-product-docs
param.type: docs/product or user-provided product docs folder

param.name: skill-contracts
param.type: .agents/skills/verify-product-docs/SKILL.md and references/*.md

param.name: orchestrator-artifacts
param.type: .tasks/product-docs-verify/<run-id>/main-orchestration.md, source-delta.md, recovery.md, aggregate scope-status.md, aggregate question-ledger.md, staging/product-verified, scopes/*/orchestrator.md, scope-local question ledgers, and worker/reviewer reports

param.name: verified-product-docs
param.type: docs/product-verified structured Markdown package

param.name: traceability
param.type: source, derivation, decision, assumption, question-ledger, and open-question maps

export-skill-instructions.PURPOSE: Define the multi-agent review and synthesis workflow.

export-output-contract.PURPOSE: Define the required `docs/product-verified` file structure and headings.

export-scope-orchestrator-contract.PURPOSE: Define the main controller, per-scope orchestrators, dispatch phases, write ownership, nested worker/reviewer loops, approval budget, interruption retry budget, source-delta handling, consistency relaunch behavior, and question-ledger workflow.

export-scaffold-script.PURPOSE: Create the managed staging verified-docs structure from a complete source inventory.

export-validate-script.PURPOSE: Validate required files, headings, placeholders, feature docs, traceability ids, evidence links, derivation confidence, and open-question sync.

target.tests: Skill quick validation, Python syntax checks, scaffold smoke test, and product-verified structure validation

notes.note-1: `docs/product` remains raw source material. `docs/product-verified` is the curated handoff package only after per-scope orchestrators record review approvals, open questions, approved derivations, source deltas, and validation evidence; staging skeletons live under `.tasks/product-docs-verify/<run-id>/staging/product-verified`; interrupted or stalled scope-orchestrators, including consistency, are relaunched with the same RUN_ID while retry budget remains instead of direct controller synthesis, and absent artifact families such as API contracts become consolidated blockers rather than invented detail.

contract.purpose: Provide the project-local Codex skill that consumes `docs/product-verified`, runs technical scope-orchestrator review loops, records answered-question source deltas, and writes `docs/technical-verified` with status `approved-to-dev` only when no blocking technical questions or answer-spawned blockers remain.

param.name: verified-product-docs
param.type: docs/product-verified or user-provided verified PRD package

param.name: skill-contracts
param.type: .agents/skills/verify-technical-docs/SKILL.md, references/*.md, scripts/*.py, and agents/openai.yaml

param.name: question-loop-artifacts
param.type: .tasks/technical-docs-verify/<run-id>/main-orchestration.md, source-delta.md, recovery.md, aggregate scope-status.md, aggregate question-ledger.md, staging/technical-verified, scopes/*/orchestrator.md, scope-local question ledgers, and worker/reviewer reports

param.name: verified-technical-docs
param.type: docs/technical-verified structured Markdown package with status questions-open, blocked, or approved-to-dev

param.name: technical-question-ledger
param.type: Traceable technical questions, answer effects, parent follow-up links, decisions, deferrals, and validation evidence

export-skill-instructions.PURPOSE: Define the technical controller workflow, question-loop rules, and final response shape.

export-output-contract.PURPOSE: Define the required `docs/technical-verified` file structure, status model, stable ids, question ledger, and approval gate.

export-scope-orchestrator-contract.PURPOSE: Define technical review scopes, nested worker/reviewer loops, answer-effect checks, write ownership, retry policy, and prompt templates.

export-scaffold-script.PURPOSE: Create the managed staging technical-verified structure from a verified product source inventory.

export-validate-script.PURPOSE: Validate required files, headings, placeholders, status values, and approved-to-dev question invariants.

export-openai-metadata.PURPOSE: Expose display name, short description, and default prompt for Codex skill surfaces.

target.tests: Skill quick validation, Python syntax checks, scaffold smoke test, technical-verified structure validation, XML validation, and GRACE lint

notes.note-1: The skill intentionally writes a `questions-open` package when blockers remain so the questions can be answered in the next loop; it may write `approved-to-dev` only after every answer delta is reviewed and consistency confirms no new blocking follow-up question exists.

contract.purpose: Provide the project-local Codex skill that consumes raw PRD, verified PRD, and optional technical readiness docs, runs subagent scope-mapper and reviewer loops, and writes a shallow backend-only implementation wave map plus per-page frontend handoff files without detailing individual waves.

param.name: raw-product-docs
param.type: docs/product or user-provided raw PRD source folder used for frontend page extraction

param.name: verified-product-docs
param.type: docs/product-verified or user-provided verified PRD package

param.name: technical-docs
param.type: docs/technical-verified when present, including open blockers or approved-to-dev status

param.name: skill-contracts
param.type: .agents/skills/decompose-prd-waves/SKILL.md, references/*.md, scripts/*.py, and agents/openai.yaml

param.name: decomposition-artifacts
param.type: .tasks/prd-wave-decomposition/<run-id>/main-orchestration.md, source-gate.md, source-delta.md, staging/prd-waves, scopes/*/orchestrator.md, mapper reports, reviewer reports, scope-local ledgers, aggregate ledgers, and recovery.md

param.name: prd-waves
param.type: docs/prd-waves structured Markdown package with shallow backend-only wave map, frontend-pages/index.md, frontend-pages/page-<nnn>.md files, and user approval state

param.name: wave-map-handoff
param.type: Backend wave ids, names, outcomes, included/excluded backend scope, dependency order, frontend page file index, recommended next planning surfaces, reviewer verdicts, open questions, and traceability

export-skill-instructions.PURPOSE: Define source gates, full-PRD shallow decomposition, subagent scope mapping, reviewer approval, user approval pause, and final response shape.

export-output-contract.PURPOSE: Define the required `docs/prd-waves` structure, status model, stable ids, reviewer verdicts, question ledger, backend-only wave gate, per-page frontend gate, raw/verified source traceability gate, shallow gate, and user approval gate.

export-scope-orchestrator-contract.PURPOSE: Define scope-mapper, mapper worker, reviewer, consistency, write ownership, retry policy, and prompt templates.

export-scaffold-script.PURPOSE: Create the managed staging prd-waves structure from raw product, verified product, and technical source inventories.

export-validate-script.PURPOSE: Validate required files, headings, placeholders, status values, reviewer approvals, shallow-only restrictions, stable ids, frontend page files, raw/verified page traceability, and open blocking question invariants.

export-openai-metadata.PURPOSE: Expose display name, short description, and default prompt for Codex skill surfaces.

target.tests: Skill quick validation, Python syntax checks, scaffold smoke test, prd-waves structure validation, XML validation, and GRACE lint

notes.note-1: The skill intentionally produces only top-level backend waves and one source-backed frontend markdown file per page; downstream detailed planning must use a separate skill after the shallow map is reviewer-approved and, when required, user-approved.

contract.purpose: Provide the project-local Codex skill that consumes an approved shallow backend PRD wave, reads verified sources, GRACE contracts, neighboring backend waves, separate frontend-pages dependency context, and relevant code, and details exactly one selected backend wave into a ready-for-dev backend brief with AC, EC, verification obligations, and reviewer-approved fit evidence.

param.name: prd-waves
param.type: docs/prd-waves or user-provided shallow wave package containing the selected backend WAVE-<nn> and separate frontend-pages/** context

param.name: verified-product-docs
param.type: docs/product-verified or source-traceable product package used by the shallow wave map

param.name: technical-docs
param.type: docs/technical-verified when present

param.name: grace-contracts
param.type: docs/development-plan.xml, docs/knowledge-graph.xml, and docs/verification-plan.xml

param.name: codebase-context
param.type: Read-only source files and module contracts relevant to the selected wave

param.name: skill-contracts
param.type: .agents/skills/detail-prd-wave/SKILL.md, references/*.md, scripts/*.py, and agents/openai.yaml

param.name: wave-detail-artifacts
param.type: .tasks/prd-wave-detail/<run-id>/main-orchestration.md, source-wave-gate.md, context-inventory.md, source-delta.md, staging/prd-wave-details, waves/*/orchestrator.md, planner reports, reviewer reports, final fit reports, wave-local ledgers, aggregate ledgers, and recovery.md

param.name: prd-wave-details
param.type: docs/prd-wave-details structured Markdown package with exactly one selected detailed backend wave per run

param.name: selected-wave-handoff
param.type: Detailed backend implementation slices, acceptance criteria, exit criteria, verification obligations, codebase fit, neighboring-backend-wave fit, frontend-pages dependency context, reviewer verdicts, open questions, traceability, and user approval state for the selected backend wave

export-skill-instructions.PURPOSE: Define selected backend-wave source gates, one-backend-wave focus, codebase and neighboring-backend-wave context inventory, frontend-pages dependency context, nested subagent orchestration, reviewer approvals, user approval pause, and final response shape.

export-output-contract.PURPOSE: Define the required `docs/prd-wave-details` structure, status model, stable ids, reviewer verdicts, question ledger, ready-for-dev gate, and user approval gate.

export-wave-orchestrator-contract.PURPOSE: Define specialist planner and reviewer roles, final fit review, write ownership, retry policy, and prompt templates.

export-scaffold-script.PURPOSE: Create the managed staging prd-wave-details structure for one selected backend wave from shallow backend wave, product, technical, and frontend-pages context inventories.

export-validate-script.PURPOSE: Validate required files, headings, placeholders, status values, reviewer approvals, AC/EC/test ids, codebase fit, and open blocking question invariants.

export-openai-metadata.PURPOSE: Expose display name, short description, and default prompt for Codex skill surfaces.

target.tests: Skill quick validation, Python syntax checks, scaffold smoke test, prd-wave-details structure validation, XML validation, and GRACE lint

notes.note-1: The skill intentionally details exactly one selected backend wave, treats frontend-pages/** as read-only dependency context, and treats user approval as separate from reviewer-approved ready-for-dev; downstream execution or task creation must wait for explicit approval.

contract.purpose: Provide the project-local Codex skill that consumes `approved-to-dev` technical readiness docs, builds a shallow full backend wave map, and details exactly one sequential backend wave at a time until reviewer-approved, question-free, ready-for-dev, and explicitly user-approved.

param.name: approved-technical-docs
param.type: docs/technical-verified or user-provided technical package with status approved-to-dev

param.name: verified-product-docs
param.type: docs/product-verified or source-traceable product package used by the technical docs

param.name: skill-contracts
param.type: .agents/skills/plan-backend-waves/SKILL.md, references/*.md, scripts/*.py, and agents/openai.yaml

param.name: wave-orchestration-artifacts
param.type: .tasks/backend-wave-plan/<run-id>/main-orchestration.md, technical-approval-gate.md, source-delta.md, staging/backend-waves, waves/*/orchestrator.md, planner reports, reviewer reports, wave-local ledgers, aggregate ledgers, and recovery.md

param.name: backend-waves
param.type: docs/backend-waves structured Markdown package with shallow full wave map and current ready-for-dev or blocked wave

param.name: wave-handoff
param.type: Detailed backend implementation tasks, acceptance criteria, exit criteria, verification obligations, reviewer verdicts, open questions, and user approval state for the current wave

export-skill-instructions.PURPOSE: Define the technical approval gate, strict sequential wave loop, reviewer approvals, user approval pause, and final response shape.

export-output-contract.PURPOSE: Define the required `docs/backend-waves` structure, status model, stable ids, reviewer verdicts, question ledger, ready-for-dev gate, and user approval gate.

export-wave-orchestrator-contract.PURPOSE: Define wave planner/reviewer roles, required reviewer perspectives, write ownership, retry policy, and prompt templates.

export-scaffold-script.PURPOSE: Create the managed staging backend-waves structure from approved technical and product source inventories.

export-validate-script.PURPOSE: Validate required files, headings, placeholders, status values, reviewer approvals, AC/exit/test ids, and open blocking question invariants.

export-openai-metadata.PURPOSE: Expose display name, short description, and default prompt for Codex skill surfaces.

target.tests: Skill quick validation, Python syntax checks, scaffold smoke test, backend-waves structure validation, XML validation, and GRACE lint

notes.note-1: The skill intentionally separates `ready-for-dev` from user approval: a wave may be ready only after reviewer approvals and zero open blockers, but the next wave cannot be planned until the user explicitly approves the current wave overview.

contract.purpose: Provide the project-local Codex skill that converts implementation, architecture, research, PRD, or verification plans into a non-executable Beads milestone with child epics and tasks while preserving source-plan intent.

param.name: source-plan
param.type: Implementation plan, architecture document, research plan, PRD, verification plan, or equivalent source path

param.name: skill-contracts
param.type: .agents/skills/plan-to-beads/SKILL.md and agents/openai.yaml

param.name: beads-state
param.type: Existing project Beads state and conventions when task creation is requested

param.name: beads-graph
param.type: One non-executable milestone with implementation, full test coverage, and pre-MR QA child epics plus source-anchored tasks and acceptance criteria

param.name: coverage-ledger
param.type: Requirement-to-task traceability when the source plan is large or risky

export-skill-instructions.PURPOSE: Define the semantic-loss, milestone-plus-three-epic, requirement-ledger, task-content, delivery-contour, and blocker-handling rules.

export-openai-metadata.PURPOSE: Expose display name, short description, and default prompt for Codex skill surfaces.

target.tests: Skill quick validation, metadata presence checks, and focused diff whitespace checks

notes.note-1: The skill assumes Beads exists in the target project. If no source plan or Beads state is available, it should ask for the missing artifact instead of inventing scope.
notes.note-2: The skill assumes the supported Beads baseline includes a milestone type; if milestone creation fails, it should report a local tooling mismatch instead of faking milestone semantics with an epic or task.

contract.purpose: Provide the project-local Codex skill that reviews specs, design docs, implementation plans, PRDs, diffs, and other artifacts through independent subagent reviewer loops until approval or an explicit blocker.

param.name: review-target
param.type: File path, diff, pasted artifact, or latest generated artifact in conversation

param.name: source-context
param.type: User goal, requirements, docs, code/test pointers, constraints, and acceptance criteria

param.name: skill-contracts
param.type: .agents/skills/subagent-review-loop/SKILL.md and agents/openai.yaml

param.name: approved-artifact
param.type: Revised artifact approved by required subagent reviewer perspectives

param.name: resolution-log
param.type: Accepted findings, rejected findings with controller rationale, changed sections/files, and residual blockers

export-skill-instructions.PURPOSE: Define reviewer selection, subagent prompt contract, controller adjudication, artifact editing, and re-review closure rules.

export-openai-metadata.PURPOSE: Expose display name, short description, and default prompt for Codex skill surfaces.

target.tests: Skill quick validation, metadata presence checks, file-local markup checks, and focused diff whitespace checks

notes.note-1: The skill keeps the main session as controller and artifact editor. Subagents are read-only reviewers; the controller validates each finding, rejects invalid comments with rationale, and re-runs affected reviewers until approval or an explicit unresolved blocker.

DF-LOCAL-DEV.NAME: LocalDevelopmentStartup
DF-LOCAL-DEV.step-1: Start PostgreSQL and Redis using `docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.dev.yml up -d`.
DF-LOCAL-DEV.step-2: Run `bun run dev` to start the Go API on `8090`, Vite admin SPA on `3100`, and Next public web app on `3101`.
DF-LOCAL-DEV.step-3: Run `nx serve bot` separately only when Telegram bot development is needed and `BOT_TOKEN` is configured.
DF-LOCAL-DEV.evidence: API health response, web-admin page load at `/users`, public web page load, and no startup errors in terminal output.

DF-USER-GRAPHQL.NAME: AdminUserGraphQLFlow
DF-USER-GRAPHQL.TRIGGER: Admin user domain UI or GraphQL operation
DF-USER-GRAPHQL.step-1: Web-admin client sends typed GraphQL operation generated from the admin schema with browser credentials included.
DF-USER-GRAPHQL.step-2: API GraphQL handler validates admin session context, routes to generated resolver, and calls the user service.
DF-USER-GRAPHQL.step-3: Service validates input, performs persistence work, and returns typed success or error union.
DF-USER-GRAPHQL.evidence: Focused API tests, web-admin tests, schema validation, and generated typecheck pass.

DF-WEB-ADMIN-AUTH.NAME: WebAdminBackendAuthFlow
DF-WEB-ADMIN-AUTH.TRIGGER: Admin login, session lookup, logout, or createAdmin operation
DF-WEB-ADMIN-AUTH.step-1: API startup applies admin env overlay/defaults, runs migrations, and seeds exactly one first admin only while `admin_users` is empty.
DF-WEB-ADMIN-AUTH.step-2: `loginAdmin` verifies credentials, creates a Redis-backed opaque session, and sets an httpOnly `/graphql` cookie through the GraphQL cookie bridge.
DF-WEB-ADMIN-AUTH.step-3: Admin GraphQL requests pass credentialed CORS, origin guard, session middleware, and resolver guards before protected user/admin operations execute.
DF-WEB-ADMIN-AUTH.step-4: `logoutAdmin` revokes the Redis session and clears the cookie; `me` returns the current active admin or null.
DF-WEB-ADMIN-AUTH.evidence: Admin auth service, repository, Redis, middleware, GraphQL, web-admin transport, startup smoke, and e2e checks pass.

DF-USER-REST.NAME: PublicUserRESTFlow
DF-USER-REST.TRIGGER: Public web user domain UI or REST operation
DF-USER-REST.step-1: Browser code sends same-origin REST requests to the Next `/api/users` route proxy using React Query.
DF-USER-REST.step-2: Next route proxy and server code resolve `WEB_API_BASE_URL` and forward requests to the Go API REST handler.
DF-USER-REST.step-3: API REST handler maps HTTP request and response envelopes to the shared user service.
DF-USER-REST.step-4: Service validates input, performs persistence work, and returns transport-neutral success or domain errors.
DF-USER-REST.evidence: Focused API REST handler tests, web REST client tests, web typecheck/build, and web e2e pass.

DF-CODEGEN.NAME: ApiAndWebCodegenFlow
DF-CODEGEN.TRIGGER: SQL query, migration, or GraphQL schema changes
DF-CODEGEN.step-1: Validate `libs/graphql/schema/*.graphql` and keep `apps/api/sqlc.yaml` aligned with goose migrations plus repository query SQL.
DF-CODEGEN.step-2: Run API sqlc generation and API gqlgen generation.
DF-CODEGEN.step-3: Run web-admin GraphQL codegen and typecheck.
DF-CODEGEN.evidence: `bun run codegen`, `bunx nx run codegen:validate`, and `bunx nx run web-admin:typecheck` complete without drift.

DF-COVERAGE-GATE.NAME: CoverageGateFlow
DF-COVERAGE-GATE.TRIGGER: Developer or CI enters explicit coverage, release, or template handoff gate
DF-COVERAGE-GATE.step-1: Run `tools/coverage/preflight.mjs` to validate thresholds, configured profiles, summaries, and allowlist entries.
DF-COVERAGE-GATE.step-2: Run Go coverage profiles for API, bot, go-config, and go-logger, filtering only the explicit Go allowlist.
DF-COVERAGE-GATE.step-3: Run web-admin, web, and workspace tooling Vitest coverage, then compare coverage summaries to 100 percent thresholds for this explicit coverage phase.
DF-COVERAGE-GATE.step-4: Run Playwright e2e with Docker Compose PostgreSQL and Redis, API on `18080`, web-admin, and web sequentially.
DF-COVERAGE-GATE.evidence: `bun run test:coverage`, `bunx nx run web-admin:e2e`, `bunx nx run web:e2e`, and `bun run verify:coverage` pass with artifacts in `dist/coverage`, `dist/test-results/web-admin-e2e`, `dist/test-results/web-e2e`, `dist/playwright-report/web-admin`, and `dist/playwright-report/web`.

DF-CI-CD-RELEASE.NAME: GitLabDokployReleaseFlow
DF-CI-CD-RELEASE.TRIGGER: Merge request, develop push, main push, or SemVer tag
DF-CI-CD-RELEASE.step-1: Merge requests run deterministic affected checks using the merge request diff base SHA.
DF-CI-CD-RELEASE.step-2: CI jobs request explicit department runner tags for fast, full, image-build, and deploy workloads.
DF-CI-CD-RELEASE.step-3: `develop` and `main` run `bun run verify:coverage` and publish coverage plus Playwright evidence.
DF-CI-CD-RELEASE.step-4: `develop` builds and pushes dev images, then updates and deploys the Dokploy dev compose stack.
DF-CI-CD-RELEASE.step-5: SemVer tags reachable from `origin/main` build immutable release images and create GitLab Release metadata.
DF-CI-CD-RELEASE.step-6: Manual `deploy:prod` updates the Dokploy production compose image variables and triggers compose deploy.
DF-CI-CD-RELEASE.evidence: GitLab pipeline status, image metadata artifact, GitLab Release, Dokploy update/deploy response summary, and confirmation that the `web` image is the public Next app while admin Vite deployment is not included unless a separate admin image/service is added.

DF-GRACE-CHANGE.NAME: GraceGovernedChange
DF-GRACE-CHANGE.step-1: Read requirements, technology, development plan, graph, verification plan, operational packet context, and existing file-local contracts where present.
DF-GRACE-CHANGE.step-2: Create or update MODULE_CONTRACT and MODULE_MAP for new or meaningfully edited governed files before changing shared behavior.
DF-GRACE-CHANGE.step-3: Apply scoped code and test changes.
DF-GRACE-CHANGE.step-4: Run focused verification, refresh GRACE artifacts, and run `grace lint --path .`.
DF-GRACE-CHANGE.evidence: Checkpoint report includes assumptions, commands, evidence, file-local contract delta, graph delta, and verification delta.

Phase-1.name: Baseline-GRACE-Migration
Phase-1.goal: Replace legacy agent-docs guidance with project-local GRACE skills and shared XML artifacts.
Phase-1.step-1: Route agent workflow through AGENTS.md, GRACE docs, and file-local contract markup rules.
Phase-1.step-2: Preserve schema as shared API contract.

Phase-2.name: Feature-Development
Phase-2.goal: Use GRACE plan, verification, execution, refresh, and review loops for future template features.
Phase-2.step-1: Use scoped module contracts for API behavior changes.
Phase-2.step-2: Use generated GraphQL client checks for web-admin changes.
Phase-2.step-3: Use REST client, Next route-proxy, typecheck, and browser checks for public web changes.
Phase-2.step-4: Use bot command tests and config checks for bot changes.
Phase-2.step-5: Use the root coverage gate only for explicit 100 percent coverage work, coverage epic closeout, template handoff, or release.
Phase-2.step-6: Use GitLab CI and Dokploy release gates for template delivery.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope.
ExecutionPolicy.replan-trigger: Missing dependency, contradictory contract, generated artifact drift, or unclear third-party surface.
ExecutionPolicy.checkpoint-requirement: Every step must leave assumptions, commands run, evidence, graph delta, verification delta, and next action.
